behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue interaction: the behavior requires both HTTP rejection and verifying no enqueue side effect. Unit tests of signature math alone can miss accidental forwarding. Rejected System because behavior text is about signature/body mismatch and forwarding logic, not real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler with a real request object and a wired queue adapter test double; send body+bad X-Signature-256, assert 401 and assert enqueue was not called/persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering contract across modules (handler + persistent queue). A realistic in-process persistent queue (local DB/filesystem) is enough to catch ack-before-write bugs. Rejected System because behavior text specifies write-before-ack ordering, not real network/subprocess variability.
behaviors.test_description: Use a test queue implementation that confirms durable-write completion via a promise; assert HTTP 200 is emitted only after the queue write resolves and that reversing order fails the test.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at handoff boundary between handler and downstream worker. Need wired modules to prove response is decoupled from worker completion. Rejected System because behavior text is about not waiting for processing, which can be validated without real network/OS.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker stub that blocks for a long time; assert response is returned immediately after enqueue and before worker completion.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time/concurrency SLO under load. In-process mocks/fake timers can hide scheduler, socket, and runtime contention failures. Real HTTP + runtime timing is the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service, generate concurrent earlier events that trigger slow processing, then send a new valid webhook over real HTTP and measure wall-clock; assert response <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on interaction between handler and persistent queue/idempotency store. Unit logic alone can miss duplicate writes caused by module wiring/state boundaries. Rejected System because behavior text is idempotent write prevention, not OS/network-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the handler with real queue persistence in test mode; assert first write succeeds and second is ignored/rejected with only one stored queue entry.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly starts from local contract: "queue reports it is full" then handler maps to HTTP 503 + header. This is module interaction; no real OS/network dependency required. Rejected System because fullness signal is injected by queue contract, not environment effects.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler wired to a queue module configured to return/full error; send webhook and assert 503 and Retry-After header value/presence.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Capacity enforcement is a queue+storage behavior best caught with real local persistence wiring (DB/filesystem) and configured limits. Rejected System because behavior text is configured capacity logic, not external network/subprocess/OS timing artifacts.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against test storage with small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection and unchanged persisted count.
